diff_user.system:
You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Food. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

Predicate: country - Example triple: (Ajoblanco | country | Spain)
Predicate: ingredient - Example triple: (Ajoblanco | ingredient | Almond)
Predicate: region - Example triple: (Ajoblanco | region | Andalusia)
Predicate: division - Example triple: (Almond | division | Flowering plant)

Predicate: order - Example triple: (Almond | order | Rosales)
Predicate: course - Example triple: (Amatriciana sauce | course | Italian meal structure)
Predicate: dishVariation - Example triple: (Amatriciana sauce | dishVariation | "Onion, garlic, black pepper, chili")
Predicate: mainIngredient - Example triple: (Amatriciana sauce | mainIngredient | "Tomatoes, guanciale, cheese, olive oil")
Predicate: leader - Example triple: (Andalusia | leader | Susana Díaz)
Predicate: alternativeName - Example triple: (Asam pedas | alternativeName | "Asam padeh")
Predicate: creatorOfDish - Example triple: (Asam pedas | creatorOfDish | Malay cuisine)

Predicate: servingTemperature - Example triple: (Bakewell pudding | servingTemperature | "Warm (freshly baked) or cold")
Predicate: carbohydrate - Example triple: (Barny Cakes | carbohydrate | 18.0 g)
Predicate: fat - Example triple: (Barny Cakes | fat | 4.8 g)
Predicate: year - Example triple: (Barny Cakes | year | 1999)
Predicate: related - Example triple: (Bhajji | related | Pakora)
Predicate: similarDish - Example triple: (Bhajji | similarDish | "Pakora and other fritters made from wheat or corn flour")

Predicate: isPartOf - Example triple: (Derbyshire Dales | isPartOf | Derbyshire)

    # Build ONE complex sentence from all triples by connecting subjects
    if not sentences:
        return ""

    # If only one subject, return its sentence
    if len(sentences) == 1:
        return sentences[0]

    # For multiple subjects, connect them into one complex sentence
    # Try to find relationships between subjects (e.g., subject1 has country subject2)
    subject_info = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_info:
            subject_info[t.subject] = {}
        subject_info[t.subject][t.predicate] = t.object

    # Find connection points between subjects
    connected_parts = []
    used_subjects = set()

    # Start with the first subject as the main topic
    first_subject = list(subject_triples.keys())[0]
    used_subjects.add(first_subject)

    # Build the main clause
    main_parts = []
    for t in subject_triples[first_subject]:
        s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object
        if p == "country":
            main_parts.append(f"{s} is from {o}")
        elif p == "region":
            main_parts.append(f"{s} originates from {o}")
        elif p == "mainIngredient":
            main_parts.append(f"with {o} as the main ingredient")
        elif p == "ingredient":
            main_parts.append(f"containing {o}")
        elif p == "dishVariation":
            main_parts.append(f"with variations like {o}")
        elif p == "course":
            main_parts.append(f"typically served as a {o}")
        elif p == "servingTemperature" or p == "served":
            main_parts.append(f"served {o}")
        elif p == "servingSize":
            main_parts.append(f"with a serving size of {o}")
        elif p == "carbohydrate":
            main_parts.append(f"containing {o} carbohydrates")
        elif p == "fat":
            main_parts.append(f"containing {o} fat")
        elif p == "protein":
            main_parts.append(f"containing {o} protein")
        elif p == "division":
            main_parts.append(f"belonging to the division {o}")
        elif p == "family":
            main_parts.append(f"in the family {o}")
        elif p == "order":
            main_parts.append(f"under the order {o}")
        elif p == "genus":
            main_parts.append(f"of the genus {o}")
        elif p == "class":
            main_parts.append(f"classified as a {o}")
        elif p == "alternativeName":
            main_parts.append(f"also known as {o}")
        elif p == "creator" or p == "creatorOfDish":
            main_parts.append(f"created by {o}")
        elif p == "year":
            main_parts.append(f"introduced in {o}")
        elif p == "related":
            main_parts.append(f"related to {o}")
        elif p == "similarDish":
            main_parts.append(f"similar to {o}")
        elif p == "isPartOf":
            main_parts.append(f"part of {o}")
        elif p == "capital":
            main_parts.append(f"where the capital is {o}")
        elif p == "leader":
            main_parts.append(f"led by {o}")
        elif p == "currency":
            main_parts.append(f"using {o} as currency")
        elif p == "demonym":
            main_parts.append(f"with {o}")
        elif p == "ethnicGroup":
            main_parts.append(f"home to {o}")
        elif p == "language":
            main_parts.append(f"where {o} is spoken")
        elif p == "officialLanguage":
            main_parts.append(f"with {o} as the official language")
        elif p == "leaderTitle":
            main_parts.append(f"with the leader title {o}")
        elif p == "foundationPlace":
            main_parts.append(f"founded in {o}")
        else:
            main_parts.append(f"{s} {p} {o}")

    connected_parts.append(" ".join(main_parts))

    # Find connected subjects and add them with relative clauses
    for subj in subject_triples.keys():
        if subj == first_subject or subj in used_subjects:
            continue

        # Check if this subject is referenced by the first subject
        for t in subject_triples[first_subject]:
            if t.object == subj:
                # This subject is connected via the first subject's predicate
                rel_parts = []
                for t2 in subject_triples[subj]:
                    s2, p2, o2 = t2.subject, t2.predicate, t2.object
                    if p2 == "capital":
                        rel_parts.append(f"where the capital is {o2}")
                    elif p2 == "country":
                        rel_parts.append(f"which is in {o2}")
                    elif p2 == "region":
                        rel_parts.append(f"which is in {o2}")
                    elif p2 == "currency":
                        rel_parts.append(f"which uses {o2}")
                    elif p2 == "language":
                        rel_parts.append(f"where {o2} is spoken")
                    else:
                        rel_parts.append(f"with {o2}")

                if rel_parts:
                    connected_parts.append(f" and {subj}, {' '.join(rel_parts)}")
                used_subjects.add(subj)
                break

    # Join all parts into one sentence
    sentence = "".join(connected_parts).strip()
    if not sentence.endswith("."):
        sentence += "."

    return sentence
